programmes.show_description: The Assault on Choga Sar - Part 1. 1974. Stars William Mervyn, John Flanagan & David Ashford. Two mountaineers died in an unsuccessful attempt to scale the Choga Sar in the Himalayas.
The Assault on Choga Sar - Part 2. 1974. Stars Keith Buckley, John Flanagan & Kenneth Cope. Captain Bowman claims that Wainwright lied about Nielsen dying in a fall...
The Assault on Choga Sar - Part 3. 1974. Stars William Mervyn, John Flanagan & Keith Buckley. Lloyd accuses the group of blaming Wainwright for Bowman's failures that lead to the two deaths.
1965. Crime. Directed by Arthur Penn. Stars Warren Beatty, Alexandra Stewart & Hurd Hatfield. A stand-up comic incurs the wrath of the Mafia and escapes to Chicago but yearns to go back on stage.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life The Sweet Life
programmes.show_description: 1966. An exciting glimpse into Britain's colourful confectionery industry.
1963. Who Killed Cynthia Royal? Directed by Charles F Haas. Stars Gene Barry, Gary Conway & Regis Toomey. Burke has to search for a missing cat before he can find a killer.
1960. Stella. Stars Michael Ansara, Gloria Talbott & Richard Devon. Sam becomes friends with a wanted female bank robber who is unaware that he is a deputy marshal.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life The Sinking City
programmes.show_description: 1968. An intriguing look at the efforts being made to prevent Venice from sinking into the mud.
1941. Romance. Directed by Julien Duvivier. Stars Merle Oberon, Joseph Cotton & Edna May Oliver. Wealthy Lydia Macmillan meets three former suitors and together they relive the past thirty years.
1948. Drama. Director: Fergus McDonell. Stars. James Donald, Valerie Hobson, David Greene, Howard Keel. A couple come across a car crash, where they discover that the occupants are armed.
1960. Factual. Directed by James Ritchie. The 'Blue Pullman', a new diesel train liaising between Manchester and St. Pancras is prepared for its grand inaugural journey.
1949. Drama. Directors Michael Powell, Emeric Pressburger. Stars David Farrar, Jack Hawkins and Kathleen Byron. The best bomb disposal expert must battle his inner self, during the German raids.
Flat Two. 1961. Directed by Alan Cooke. Stars John Le Mesurier, Jack Watling & Barry Keegan. Two men visit the apartment of a rich gambler - Each with a strong motive for murder!
The Coming of Robin Hood. 1955. Stars Richard Greene, Victor Woolf & Alan Wheatley. When Robin of Locksley returns home from the Holy Land, he finds Normans are in control everywhere.
1961. Drama. Directed by Quentin Lawrence. Stars Peter Cushing, Andre Morell & Richard Vernon. A crook abducts the wife and child of a bank manager and then schemes to rob the institution.
1951. A magnificent travelogue about Soho, featuring the streets, businesses and people you would find in this area of London in the 1950s.
The Weasel. 1960. Stars John Ireland, Miki Iveria & Dawn Beret. On the eve of Madame Orlette's fashion show a fire destroys all of her dresses. Could it have been a rival or someone closer?
1948. Mystery drama. Directed by David MacDonald and starring Dennis Price, Herbert Lom and Robert Newton. A group of people search for Nazi treasure hidden in the Alps.
A magnificent short film produced by and about the L.C.C. Men's Institute in Battersea, which looks at the various activities on offer, including fitness classes, bee keeping and photography.
Information Received. 1962. Stars Diane Aubrey, John Barrie & Micheal Barrington. Things do not go to plan when Stephen Peake stages a break in, in the hope that Sergeant Watt will be sued.
1969. Detective & Mystery Drama. Welcome To Brighton? Stars John Bindon, Alfred Burke & Heather Canning. A Private Inquiry Agent is released from prison on parole and attempts to build a new life.
Caroline Munro opens the Cellar Club tonight with an episode of Supernatural: Viktoria. This episode stars Judy Cornwell, Lewis Fiander and Catherine Schell.
Viktoria. 1977. Stars Judy Cornwell, Lewis Fiander, Norman Eshley & Catherine Schell. After her mother's suspicious death, a young girl becomes obsessed with an unusual new doll.
Up next at the Cellar Club Caroline Munro has two things to share! First we have an episode of Tales of Tomorrow starring James Dean. After this, watch as Kim Newman hosts the Cellar Club Live!
The Evil Within. 1953. Stars James Dean, Margaret Phillips & Rod Steiger. A scientist's neglected wife is accidentally dosed with a chemical that unleashes a beast within.
Hosted by horror aficionado Kim Newman in front of a live audience, hear 'horror' stories from Caroline Munro, Virginia Wetherell and sons of the legendary Brian Clemens, Samuel & George Clemens.
After tonights special show, Caroline Munro is here to tell us what is coming up next week at the Cellar Club.

programmes.show_name: Hammer House of Mystery and Suspense
programmes.show_description: The Sweet Scent of Death. 1984. Stars Dean Stockwell & Shirley Knight. A US ambassador takes his wife to a beautiful country home, but their trip quickly turns into a nightmare.
1973. Comedy. Day-dreamer, Albert (David Jason) has hopes of being a superhero and saving the girls.
1967. Western. Director: James Neilson. Stars Roddy McDowall, Suzanne Pleshette & Karl Malden. To restore his family's wealth, a young lad stows away on a ship bound for the California Gold Rush.
See the Armstrong Siddeley, Morris Oxford, Dennis & Bedford Fire engines, speedway and much more as we look at how high-tenacity rayon cords enables tyres to withstand the stresses of a 'modern' car.
Big Skate/Pannage. 1986. Jack Hargreaves recounts a trip to Shetland in search of large fish. In his second film, he witnesses pigs dealing with a bumper crop of acorns.

programmes.show_name: The Cisco Kid: South of Rio Grande
programmes.show_description: 1945. Western. Director Lambert Hillyer. Stars Duncan Renaldo, Martin Garralaga & Armida. Cisco and Pancho infiltrate the operations of Miguel Sanches and free the district from his control.
1949. A charming puppet animation film about road safety.
The Adventure of the Holy Finger. 1974. Stars Robert Russell, Edward Brayshaw & Deddie Davies. A crazed woman summons Sir Richard and Peterkin to help strange travellers who carry treasure.
1997. Stars Brendan Pollecutt, Fezile Mpela & Robert Finlayson. Ram is banished to the outlands by the king after losing a battle with Dean, and begins plotting his revenge.
The Ruby of Radnor. 1957. Stars: William Russell, Cyril Smith & Ronald Leigh-Hunt. As punishment Squire Brian is sent on an errand delivering a crate of pigeons to a neighbouring castle.
Enter Batgirl, Exit Penguin. 1967. Stars Adam West, Burt Ward & Yvonne Craig. Batman and Robin find out just how valuable their new ally is when they are hopelessly strung up in Penguin's lair.
The Big Chance. 1955. Stars Roy Rogers, Trigger & Dale Evans. Pat is kidnapped by an outlaw gang, one of whom may be in line for a million dollar inheritance.
Manhunt. 1967. Stars Paul Maxwell, Janna Hill & Francis Matthews. The hunt is on to apprehend Captain Black, after he inadvertently becomes a radioactive hotspot...
Landslide. 1959. Stars: Conrad Phillips, Willoughby Goddard & Jennifer Jayne. Tell comes face to face with his exact double - an Austrian imported by Gessler to impersonate the resistance leader.
Marooned. 1956. Stars Robert Shaw, Bill Owen & Peter Hammond. Shortly after capturing Captain Clip West, the tables turn, resulting in Tempest and his men being marooned on an island.
The Thorkil Ghost. 1956. Stars Richard Greene, Victor Woolf & Barbara Mullen. A young man tells Robin of hidden treasure in a castle that his late father had an encounter with a Viking ghost.
1953. Sci-fi. Directors: Ford Beebe & Saul Goodkind. Stars Buster Crabbe, Constance Moore & Jackie Moran. Buck Rogers and his friend Buddy wake up 500 years in the future to an outlaw ruled world.
1959. Drama. Director: J. Lee Thompson. Stars Kenneth More, Lauren Bacall & Herbert Lom. In British India, a prince must be taken to safety across a rebel-held territory.
The Actress. 1961. Stars Peter Graves, Jennifer Jayne & Lew Luton. An attractive actress is kidnapped by Mike Upton. Cobb tries to rescue her until they run into some angry gold miners.
1954. Drama. Director: David Lean. Stars Charles Laughton, John Mills & Brenda de Banzie. Henry Hobson's three daughters want to marry, but he refuses as traditions require him to pay settlements.

programmes.show_name: Look At Life Down to Earth
programmes.show_description: 1966. An exciting look at some of the girls of the WRAF who trekked into the jungle, and who also take part in a jungle survival exercise, parachute jumps, and sub-aqua diving.
The Amateurs. 1962. Stars Rupert Davies, Ewen Solon & Helen Shingler. Maigret holds the suspicion that a recently released criminal is responsible for a number of daring holdups in Paris.
A Present Sovereign. 1976. Stars Gemma Jones, Christopher Cazenove & Donald Burton. A determined and kind hearted woman embarks on her career to be the best cook in England.

programmes.show_name: Play of the Month: Robinson Crusoe
programmes.show_description: 1974. Drama. Director: James MacTaggart. Stars Stanley Baker, Ram John Holder & Jerome Willis. A man struggles to survive after being shipwrecked on a deserted island.
1980. Drama. Directed by Randal Kleiser. Stars Christopher Atkins, Brooke Shields & Leo McKern. Two children are stranded on an enchanting island and experience the mysteries of adolescence.
